learning_objectives.text: Compute the cardinal value of a set.
Differentiate between equal and equivalent sets.
Differentiate between finite and infinite sets.
Represent sets in a variety of ways.
Represent well-defined sets and the empty set with proper set notation.

sets.cardinality.infinite.name: Cardinality of infinite sets (ℵ₀)
sets.cardinality.infinite.learning_objective: Differentiate between finite and infinite sets.

items.stem_html: Let S = {<mark class="param">n^2</mark> | n is a member of ℕ}. What is the cardinal value of set S?
items.answer: ℵ₀ (aleph-null) -- S is countably infinite
options: undefined -- cardinality only applies to finite sets
items.notes: Correctness follows from injectivity of the chosen formula on N, which holds for all three formula kinds used here by construction.

sets.method_choice.infinite_setbuilder.name: Best representation: infinite sets (set-builder)
sets.method_choice.infinite_setbuilder.learning_objective: Represent sets in a variety of ways.

items.stem_html: Represent the set of all real numbers using set-builder notation, and explain in one or two sentences why the roster method (even with an ellipsis) is not a practical way to represent this set.
items.answer: {x | x is a real number}. Explanation: the real numbers are uncountably infinite and have no natural 'next' element to establish a listing pattern, so no roster/ellipsis listing can represent them.
items.notes: Essay, manually graded. Rubric: set-builder correct (1 pt), explanation references uncountability/no listing pattern (1 pt).

sets.method_choice.empty_set_trick.name: Best representation: empty-set trick cases
sets.method_choice.empty_set_trick.learning_objective: Represent well-defined sets and the empty set with proper set notation.

items.stem_html: Represent the set of all triangles that are also perfect circles, symbolically.
items.notes: qti_support_level=requires_conversion: exact-match short answer needs an accepted-answers list (∅, {}, 'empty set', 'null set') configured on import.

items.stem_html: Represent the set of natural numbers divisible by zero, symbolically.
items.notes: Second empty-set-trick instance, pairs with C-02 -- this one hinges on division-by-zero being undefined rather than a geometric impossibility.

items.stem_html: Represent the set of polar bears that live in Antarctica, symbolically.
items.notes: Third empty-set-trick instance -- this one hinges on a real-world biogeography fact rather than pure logic or an undefined operation, so it needs a fact-check, not just a logic-check.

sets.method_choice.finite_word.name: Best representation: small finite sets
sets.method_choice.finite_word.learning_objective: Represent sets in a variety of ways.

items.stem_html: Determine the best way to represent the set of vowels in the word "<mark class="param">mississippi</mark>", and write the set using that method.
items.answer: {i}  (roster method -- small, specific, finite set of letters)
items.notes: vowels_in_word computed programmatically from `word` to guarantee the answer key is correct even though this item is hand-authored.

items.stem_html: Determine the best way to represent the set of Mike and Carol's children on the TV show The Brady Bunch, and write the set using that method.
items.answer: {Greg, Marcia, Peter, Jan, Bobby, Cindy}  (roster method -- small, specific, finite set of named children)
items.notes: Second instance of the small-finite-named-list method-choice family, pairs with C-03 (mississippi vowels). This is the item that used to be mislabeled as exercise 15 while actually testing a different word (mississippi) -- now the actual Brady Bunch content exists too.

items.stem_html: Determine the best way to represent the set of the seven colors commonly listed in a rainbow, and write the set using that method.
items.answer: {red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, violet}  (roster method -- small, specific, finite set of colors)
items.notes: Third instance of the small-finite-named-list method-choice family.

sets.method_choice.infinite_roster.name: Best representation: infinite sets (ellipsis roster)
sets.method_choice.infinite_roster.learning_objective: Represent sets in a variety of ways.

items.stem_html: Determine the best way to represent the set of all integers, and write it using that method. Include an ellipsis.
items.answer: {..., -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, ...}  (roster method with a leading AND trailing ellipsis, since the integers are unbounded in both directions)
items.notes: Pairs with C-01 to give the #13-20 block two different infinite-set representation strategies (set-builder vs. double-ended-ellipsis roster), plus C-02 (empty) and C-03 (finite) -- covers all four flavors an assignment spec might ask for.

learning_objectives.text: Represent sets and set relationships using Venn diagrams.

venn.region_shading.name: Venn — Region Shading
venn.region_shading.learning_objective: Represent sets and set relationships using Venn diagrams.

items.stem_html: In the Venn diagram below, sets A and B represent Jazz and Blues respectively. Shade the region representing elements outside both A and B. (Describe or identify the shaded region.)
items.answer: elements in neither A nor B
items.notes: Diagram is parameterized; Canvas may need manual Venn upload or image embed.

items.answer: elements in neither A nor B
items.notes: Diagram is parameterized; Canvas may need manual Venn upload or image embed.

items.answer: elements in neither A nor B

items.answer: elements in B only

items.stem_html: In the Venn diagram below, sets A and B represent Cats and Dogs respectively. Shade the region representing B. (Describe or identify the shaded region.)
items.answer: elements in B only

items.answer: elements in A only
items.notes: Diagram is parameterized; Canvas may need manual Venn upload or image embed.

items.stem_html: In the Venn diagram below, sets A and B represent Soccer and Basketball respectively. Shade the region representing B. (Describe or identify the shaded region.)
items.answer: elements in B only

venn.identify_elements.name: Venn — Identify Elements
venn.identify_elements.learning_objective: Represent sets and set relationships using Venn diagrams.

items.stem_html: Set A = {cricket, hockey, tape} and B = {indigo, yellow, tape}. Which elements belong to B?
items.answer: indigo, yellow, tape

items.answer: saw, level, level

items.answer: level, saw, bear

items.answer: wrench, tape, fox

items.stem_html: Set A = {golf, rugby, owl} and B = {saw, wrench, owl}. Which elements belong to B?
items.answer: saw, wrench, owl

items.answer: cricket

items.answer: banana, grape, polo

items.answer: wrench

items.stem_html: Set A = {violet, blue, indigo} and B = {violet, green, indigo}. Which elements belong to A ∩ B?
items.answer: indigo

items.answer: { fox }

items.stem_html: Given universal set U = {<mark class="param">orange</mark>, <mark class="param">violet</mark>, <mark class="param">blue</mark>, <mark class="param">red</mark>, <mark class="param">green</mark>} and A = {<mark class="param">orange</mark>, <mark class="param">red</mark>}, list the elements of A′ (the complement of A).

sets.operations.two_set_intersection.name: Operations — Two Set Intersection
sets.operations.two_set_intersection.learning_objective: Perform set operations with two sets.

items.stem_html: Let A = {<mark class="param">dog</mark>, <mark class="param">fox</mark>, <mark class="param">wolf</mark>, <mark class="param">bear</mark>, <mark class="param">owl</mark>} and B = {<mark class="param">golf</mark>, <mark class="param">hockey</mark>, <mark class="param">cricket</mark>}. Find A ∩ B.

Let A = {<mark class="param">wrench</mark>, <mark class="param">hammer</mark>, <mark class="param">level</mark>, <mark class="param">drill</mark>, <mark class="param">pliers</mark>} and B = {<mark class="param">apple</mark>, <mark class="param">cherry</mark>, <mark class="param">banana</mark>, <mark class="param">date</mark>}. Find A ∩ B.
items.answer: {cricket, rugby}

items.stem_html: Let A = {<mark class="param">dog</mark>, <mark class="param">owl</mark>, <mark class="param">fox</mark>, <mark class="param">bear</mark>} and B = {<mark class="param">grape</mark>, <mark class="param">apple</mark>, <mark class="param">cherry</mark>}. Find A ∩ B.
